SOCIAL SECURITY

One Decision. A Lifetime of Impact.

When you claim Social Security can mean a difference of hundreds of thousands of dollars over your lifetime. This decision is permanent — and most people get it wrong.

GET YOUR SS ANALYSIS →
"The difference between the right and wrong claiming age can be six figures over your lifetime." — Mike Stevens, IAR

Key Facts

1 Analysis Claiming strategy optimization
2 Spousal Benefits Included
3 Break-even Analysis Included
4 Coordination With FERS/pension if applicable
5 Cost Included

THE PROBLEM

Does This Sound Familiar?

You're guessing when to claim

Should you take it at 62? Wait until 67? Delay until 70? Every article says something different, and you're paralyzed by the decision.

You don't know your spousal options

Married couples have claiming strategies that can add tens of thousands to lifetime benefits — but most advisors don't explain them.

You're leaving money on the table

Claiming early feels safe, but it permanently reduces your benefit by up to 30%. That's a huge opportunity cost.

You haven't modeled the tax impact

Social Security is taxable income. The timing of your claim affects your tax bracket, Medicare costs, and withdrawal strategy.

THE SOLUTION

Strategic Social Security Planning

Maximize Your Lifetime Benefits

We analyze your entire financial picture — not just Social Security in isolation — to determine the optimal claiming age for you and your spouse. This includes break-even analysis, tax impact, Medicare IRMAA thresholds, and coordination with your other income sources.

The result: a clear recommendation backed by data, showing you exactly when to claim and why.

$100K+ Additional lifetime benefits for couples who optimize claiming

Our Analysis Includes:

  • Detailed review of your earnings record and benefit estimates
  • Break-even analysis for different claiming ages
  • Spousal benefit optimization for married couples
  • Survivor benefit planning and claiming strategies
  • Divorced spouse benefits eligibility and timing
  • Tax impact analysis (how SS affects your tax bracket)
  • Medicare IRMAA threshold coordination
  • Bridge income strategies if you delay claiming

How it Works

It's just three steps

BECOME A CLIENT
Step 1) Gather Your Data ▾

Request your Social Security statement and review your earnings history and projected benefits.

Step 2) Model the Scenarios ▾

Analyze claiming at 62, 67, 70, and various spousal strategies to identify the optimal approach.

Step 3) Execute the Strategy ▾

Integrate your claiming decision into your retirement income plan and tax strategy.

COMMON QUESTIONS

Social Security FAQ

Get answers to the most common Social Security questions.

Schedule SS Review
